summary:
Claim: The average apical foramen diameter in 18–25-year-old adults is approximately 502 µm.  
Evidence: - Literature shows a consistent mean range of 470–530 µm in young adults; studies like Kato et al. (2019) initially suggested age-related increases post-30. – Recent meta-analyses challenge this trend, showing negligible changes across adult ages due to methodological variation, not biological growth.  
Answer: B
